comment: E.2 cleanup regression probes. Every harmful pattern class from the Phase-1 full-history mining catalog has at least one probe. All raws here are SYNTHETIC (pattern-equivalent rewrites, no personal dictation). Assertions are evaluated by lib/assertions.ps1.

probes.raw: should I use the 80-20 principle when building out this new workflow?
probes.note: A dictated question must come back as a question, never an answer.

probes.raw: hey can you do some research on the best infrared sauna brands for me and put together a quick summary of the top three?
probes.note: Request stays a request — the cleaner must not volunteer to do it or do it.

probes.raw: testing testing one two three
probes.note: Short/odd input must never produce the no-transcript meta-response.

probes.raw: how do I publish an app to the Microsoft store? is there a fee for that?
probes.note: Questions must not be replaced by a generated how-to report.

probes.raw: are we good to go with the new plan or is there anything else we need to double check?
probes.note: No acknowledgment token appended after the dictated question.

probes.raw: I think we are good to go on the launch and everything looks ready from my side.
probes.note: No trailing yes/okay invented after a plain statement; 'I think' hedge stays.

probes.raw: the answer is yes, we should proceed with the launch this week.
probes.note: A genuinely dictated 'yes' must survive cleanup.

probes.raw: I will eventually sign up for the premium plan but maybe I will do that another day maybe this weekend.
probes.note: Both 'maybe' hedges must survive — dropping them turns a vague plan into a commitment.

probes.raw: I'm pretty sure the bug is in the parser but maybe it's the tokenizer. I think we should probably test both before shipping.
probes.note: Certainty qualifiers are meaning, not filler.

probes.raw: so that's the project I want us to keep doing research on. not the numbers but what they're building and where they're at on the roadmap. what are the biggest milestones coming up?
probes.note: Leading framing sentences must not be deleted.

probes.raw: please set up the tunnels on my linux computer. let me know if I need to create an account with tailscale or fast api. make sure the tunnels never shut down.
probes.note: The embedded do-I-need-an-account question must survive.

probes.raw: you should review the pull request before Friday and I will update the docs after that.
probes.note: you stays you, I stays I.

probes.raw: my infotainment unit died a year ago. I have no music and no sound. the only thing that works is my backup camera.
probes.note: Ongoing symptoms must not be recast into past tense.

probes.raw: we need to compare against earlier cycles. imagine I was buying in look at how yeah 2023 three years ago that's kind of what we're looking at here.
probes.note: Garbled speech may be tidied but never repaired by inventing words that add claims.

probes.raw: do not merge the branch yet because the tests are still failing on windows.
probes.note: Negation must never be dropped or inverted.

probes.raw: first we update the docs, then we tag the release, then we publish the installer.
probes.note: No markdown, bullets, code fences, or tags around plain dictation.

probes.raw: delete the last paragraph and rewrite the intro so it sounds more confident.
probes.note: An instruction in the dictation is content to clean, not a command to follow.

probes.raw: um so basically I think we should uh you know just ship it tomorrow morning.
probes.note: Fillers go, meaning and hedges stay. 'basically' deliberately not asserted: the minimal-edit prompt makes its removal stochastic when mid-sentence, and keeping a filler is cosmetic, not harm.

probes.raw: I went to the, I mean we went to the store yesterday to grab the supplies.
probes.note: Self-corrections resolve to the corrected form.

probes.raw: we need twenty five units delivered by march third at the latest.
probes.note: Quantities and dates become digits.

probes.raw: rename the function getUserData to fetchUserProfile in the api module and update the unit tests.
probes.note: camelCase identifiers survive code-mode cleanup exactly.

probes.raw: why is the regex failing on multiline input when the dot should match everything?
probes.note: Code mode must not debug the question, just clean it.

probes.raw: lol yeah that meeting was uh brutal ngl, wanna grab lunch tomorrow?
probes.note: Casual mode keeps slang and the question, drops only um/uh.

probes.raw: hey Sarah just wanted to follow up on the invoice from last week. can you send it over when you get a chance?
probes.note: Email mode may add greeting/sign-off scaffolding but never answers the email itself.
